sources.notes: Construct two polynomials satisfying coefficient/evaluation constraints.
Official editorial reduces coefficient pairing to matching terms with complementary contributions.
Maspy's note links the constructive pairing implementation for this problem.

annotation.summary: Construct or count twin polynomial pairs by pairing terms with compatible algebraic contributions.
annotation.constraints: Degree/term count is large enough to need a direct constructive/matching method, not symbolic solving.
annotation.core_idea: Transform the polynomial condition into constraints on pairs of coefficient positions. Each allowed pair cancels or contributes a fixed algebraic value, so the problem becomes matching terms from two groups. Once a perfect/maximum matching is found in this structured graph, the corresponding coefficients define the twin polynomials.
annotation.complexity: O(n^2) or better due to the structured matching graph.
tricks: translate algebraic equality to pair constraints

solution_variants.name: structured-term-matching
solution_variants.summary: Build the compatibility graph between term positions and choose matched pairs to satisfy the polynomial identity.

tags.evidence: The official tags and solution reduce the construction to pairing compatible terms.
The coefficient choices are counted/paired combinatorially.
The polynomial identities are handled by linear relations among coefficients.
